stages.rationale: Greenfield integration service with real external dependencies (Slack, MCP, Kiro inference) needs structured requirements including the inference-provider abstraction and NFRs.
Decompose requirements into implementable stories for the bot's question-answer flows.
Identify logical building blocks: Slack adapter, agent core, inference provider, MCP tool client.
Group building blocks into deployable units. contract-design will be added back here if the design splits into multiple units.
Detailed agent loop logic, Slack message/interaction design, MCP tool orchestration, inference-provider interface.
Quality targets (latency, cost, rate limits), tech stack, and patterns for the agent service.
Map components to runtime/infra and resolve how Kiro inference is invoked at deployment.
Generate the production bot service with write-test-verify cycles.

stages.skip-reason: Fully greenfield — no existing codebase or reference implementation to study (human answered Q1: No).
Slack is the interface; message/interaction formatting folds into functional-design.
Expected single deployable unit. Will be added back at units-generation if the design splits into multiple units.
